arts.ttl: Championship boss wowed by Leeds United loanee and drops fresh transfer hint
arts.sum: It remains unclear if City will look to sign Gelhardt on a permanent basis in the summer. Gelhardt has operated out wide and in the No 10 position in City head coach Ruben Selles' fluid formation.

arts.ttl: After Kristensen sale, only Harrison has loan clause at Leeds
arts.sum: Leeds United have agreed to sell right-back Rasmus Kristensen on a permanent deal, and will now be looking to find a suitor for Jack Harrison too. Jack Harrison only loan clause player left on the books at Leeds

arts.ttl: Plymouth Argyle dealt hammer blow ahead of crucial Championship survival battles against Sheffield Wednesday, Portsmouth and Derby
arts.sum: Pompey rivals Plymouth Argyle have been dealt a telling blow heading into what can be described as a defining week in their season.

arts.ttl: Leeds United can rub salt in Sheffield United wounds after transfer 'regret'
arts.sum: When quizzed on whether Sheffield United will regret letting allowing Bogle to join Leeds, former Aston Villa midfielder Lee Hendrie told Football League World : "Yeah, I certainly think so. "I think they [Sheffield United] will be looking back at that and feeling that they had a potentially very good player on their hands.

arts.ttl: Rodon fitness, Leeds United midfield mission and Meslier revenge vs Portsmouth
arts.sum: Come through matches against Portsmouth, Millwall and Queens Park Rangers with close to maximum points and Leeds can demoralise their promotion rivals ahead of the international break. It seems clear Ao Tanaka will start the game, having done so for the past 12 Championship matches.

arts.ttl: Struijk has said why he won’t swap Leeds for Feyenoord
arts.sum: March is not the time for Leeds to think about transfers, nor entertain any clubs trying to sign their star players for the summer window. Leeds United centre-back Pascal Struijk is being linked with a move back to the Netherlands this month, with Feyenoord claimed to have initiated contact.

arts.ttl: Unpopular former Leeds United man could do Daniel Farke huge favour today
arts.sum: Leeds United don’t play until Sunday lunchtime, but will be aware of what their rivals have done by the time they kick off, with Burnley and Sheffield United in action at 3pm today. Bramall Lane plays host to two promotion-winning managers from the Blades’ recent history, as Paul Heckingbottom returns to the South Yorkshire club two-and-a-half years after securing their return to the top flight in 22/23.

arts.ttl: Any Leeds players close to a ban?
arts.sum: Jayden Bogle and Junior Firpo are the two players to have been banned for accumulating five bookings earlier in the season, and Leeds’ right-back is worryingly close to a two-match ban. Currently on nine cautions for the season, another tomorrow would see Bogle handed a two-match ban for Leeds – undoubtedly a frustrating blow given his consistency as one of Leeds’ most important players this season.

arts.ttl: Sky Sports move Leeds United’s trip to Luton Town
arts.sum: Leeds United are in the driving seat for promotion back to the Premier League now, but they are going to have to overcome multiple early kick-offs in front of the Sky Sports camera to get there. First, their trip to Luton Town, already a 12:30pm kick-off, has now been nominated for Sky Sports Football covered – Whites fans will also be keeping track for a favour from Frank Lampard, as his Coventry City side host Burnley at the same time.

arts.ttl: Why Manchester City's Erling Haaland won't be sending Portsmouth's latest signing any good-luck messages after free transfer move to Fratton Park
arts.sum: It would be safe to assume that Manchester City striker Erling Haaland won’t be sending Pompey new-boy Alexander Milosevic any good luck messages following his move to English football on Friday.
